content.text: While CI finishes, let me file the silent-lineage-outage finding — that's the systemic bug behind #823.

input.command:
cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ
gh issue create --title "Lineage went silently dark for 6 days on an expired credential — a failing orchestration poll has no user-visible signal" --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --label bug --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What happened

Prod has had **no lineage at all** for six days, and nothing told anyone.

Root cause, from App Insights: the **dbt** orchestration poll has been failing every 10 minutes with an expired ADLS SAS:

```
orchestration_poll_failed  provider=dbt
ClientAuthenticationError: AuthenticationFailed
  Signed expiry time [Mon, 06 Jul 2026 14:03:00 GMT] must be after signed start time [...]
```

The dbt provider polls `run_results.json` / `manifest.json` from the artifacts store; on a successful build it refreshes `lineage_edges` (#759). With the credential expired, that read throws, the poll aborts, and the **whole dbt-manifest lineage path is dark** — which is the *shipped* lineage path, independent of any catalog.

This is what actually made the asset lineage graph empty in prod. (The Marquez/`LineageProvider` investigation in #823 was a red herring: even a perfect catalog pull wouldn't have helped, because the manifest path — the one that works — was broken underneath it.)

## The real defect: it is invisible

The poll logs `orchestration_poll_failed` at ERROR and moves on. There is **no user-visible signal anywhere**:

- The connection still shows as configured; nothing marks it unhealthy.
- The Assets/lineage UI renders the *normal empty state* ("No lineage recorded for this asset") — **identical to "this asset genuinely has no lineage"**. A broken integration and a legitimately-isolated asset are indistinguishable.
- No alert fires. `poll_orchestration_runs` reports `errors: 3` in its own summary and still returns success.

So a feature can rot for a week and the product cheerfully reports "nothing to see here". That's the bug worth fixing — the expired SAS is just the trigger.

This is the same failure shape as the one flagged in #823 ("make 'every seed unavailable' distinguishable from 'the catalog genuinely has no lineage'"), which suggests it's a *class* of problem in the lineage/orchestration surfaces, not a one-off.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A repeatedly-failing orchestration poll surfaces **in the product**, not only in logs — e.g. connection health goes degraded (we now have a connection-health axis, #803), and/or an alert fires after N consecutive failures
- [ ] "Lineage unavailable because the source is failing" is **visually distinct** from "this asset has no lineage" in the UI — never show a clean empty state over a broken integration
- [ ] An expiring credential is surfaced *before* it expires (the SAS expiry is known at mint time)
- [ ] Regression test: a poll whose artifact read raises marks the connection unhealthy rather than silently succeeding

## Immediate ops action (separate from the fix)
Re-mint the ADLS SAS for the dbt artifacts connection and re-point the connection secret.

Found while investigating why prod showed no lineage after the #801–#805 deploy.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -1

content.text:
Let me summarize where we are, since this turn has uncovered a lot.

## You were right — the lineage is ours, and I found the actual cause

It was **not** the Marquez/dbt-ol producer gap I blamed. That was a red herring. The real cause, from App Insights:

```
orchestration_poll_failed  provider=dbt
ClientAuthenticationError: Signed expiry time [Mon, 06 Jul 2026 14:03:00 GMT] ...
```

**The ADLS SAS expired on 6 July.** DataQ therefore couldn't read `manifest.json`/`run_results.json`, the dbt provider poll has been dying every 10 minutes for **six days**, and the *shipped* dbt-manifest lineage path (#759) — the one that actually works, independent of any catalog — has been dark the whole time. Even a perfect Marquez pull wouldn't have helped, because the path underneath it was broken.

**The deeper defect (filed as #828):** it was completely invisible. The UI showed the *normal* empty state — "No lineage recorded for this asset" — which is **indistinguishable from an asset that genuinely has no lineage**. No alert, no unhealthy connection, and `poll_orchestration_runs` still reported success while logging `errors: 3`. A feature rotted for a week and the product said "nothing to see here". That's the same failure shape I flagged in #823, so it's a class of problem, not a one-off.

## Security work (PR #827, CI running)

I fixed the credential leak **at the root**, not with redaction:

- **Iceberg gets a second credential slot.** ADR 0030 assumed one secret per connection; a SQL catalog needs two (storage key + catalog DB password), which is *why* the password got smuggled into `catalog_uri`. Now `catalog_secret_name` holds a SecretStore *key name* (an identifier, safe in config), the URI ships credential-less, and the password is injected at catalog-load only.
- **A validator rejects a password in `catalog_uri` outright** — refuse it at the door rather than redact forever.
- **`asset_identity` strips credentials**, so the OL namespace can never carry one. Bonus: the identity is now **stable across a password rotation** (previously, rotating it silently *forked the asset* and orphaned its lineage/incidents).
- **Read API scrubs** any URI password from any config value, any type (defence in depth for legacy rows).
- **Migration `c9d0e1f2a3b4`** repairs poisoned rows **in place** so `assets.id` and every FK survive; tested up→down→up against real Postgres.

**My own review caught a real bug in my change:** I'd wired the catalog secret into the check-runner but *not* the profiler or comparison reader — they'd have connected with **no password**. Fixed at the right altitude: one `iceberg_credentials()` resolver every read path goes through, so none can forget. Gate: 2,122 tests, 98.22%, bandit clean.

## Still to do (in order)

1. Merge + deploy #827.
2. **Rotate the Postgres password**, store it in Key Vault, re-point the iceberg connection.
3. **Re-mint the expired ADLS SAS** → this is what actually restores lineage in prod.
4. Verify: no credential in the UI, iceberg profiling works, lineage graph populated.

Note that step 2 has a sharp edge I'm sequencing around: the migration strips the password from the connection config, so Iceberg reads will fail until I re-point it. The harness is down, so nothing is running against it in the meantime.

THEN MAKE SURE ALL THE HARNESS AND UNWANTED JOBS IN AZURE ARE STOPPED/DISABLED."*

2. **Key Technical Concepts:**

   - ADR 0034: assets keyed by OpenLineage `namespace` + `name` (unique on `(namespace, name)`; `env` is metadata, not identity).
   - ADR 0011: `ConnectionAdapter` seam — "adapters never touch the DB or SecretStore; the caller resolves the secret and hands it in".
   - ADR 0030: Iceberg native read; §3 assumed **one `secret_ref` per connection** — this assumption was the root of the credential leak.
   - ADR 0005 severity tiers; #122 operational `error`/`skip` result statuses (orthogonal to severity).
   - `LineageProvider` seam (#762) + Marquez; dbt-manifest lineage (#759); OpenLineage emission (#758, dark by default).

   - nginx SPA routing vs Vite's `dist/assets/` collision.
   - Alembic backward-compatible migrations; in-place repair to preserve FKs.

3. **Files and Code Sections:**

   - **`backend/app/services/asset_view_service.py`** (#803/#805) — `AssetSummary` gained `has_operational_error`, `has_skip`, `has_cancelled_run`; `LineageNode.depth`; new `LineageEdgeRef`; `AssetDetail.lineage_edges`.
   - **`frontend/src/components/assets/health.ts`** (#803) — new `connectionHealth()` (Errors / Degraded / Running / Cancelled / Reachable / No runs) and `suiteHealth()` (severity; "Passing" gated on `checks_total > 0`; "Partial" when cancelled; "No data").
   - **`backend/app/lineage/edges.py`** (#805) — new `lineage_neighbourhood()` + `_walk_graph()` returning nodes-with-hop-depth **and** the real traversed edges, normalized `(upstream_id, downstream_id)`.

     location ~* ^/assets/.+\.(?:js|mjs|css|map|png|jpe?g|gif|svg|ico|webp|avif|woff2?|ttf|eot)$ {
         try_files $uri =404;
         expires 1y;
         add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
     }

         add_header Cache-Control "no-cache";

   - **`backend/app/core/uri_credentials.py`** (new, #827) — `uri_password()`, `strip_uri_credentials()` (keeps username), `inject_uri_password()` (percent-encodes so `@ : /` can't repoint the URI), `redact_config_uris()`.

     ```python

I redesigned around each (PG sidecar; in-network probe job; sanitized issue text).

   **Security/handling constraints that remain in force:**
   - Never force-push an open PR; revise with a `fixup:` commit + plain push.
   - Secrets: inline-fetch only — straight from command substitution into the consuming command; never print, never write to a file.

My position: the *namespace* is the OL identity (ADR 0034) and must stay physical/stable, but the **UI should display a friendly label** (e.g. the connection name) — a presentation change worth doing.

   - **Final action: the live connection test returned `{ status: 200, body: { ok: true } }`** — proving DataQ resolves both secrets, injects the rotated password into the credential-less URI, and reaches the SQL catalog. The rotation is functionally verified.

"""dbt orchestration provider (ADR 0029) — artifact-poll + HMAC callback.

dbt Core has no runs API. DataQ observes dbt builds through their **universal
surface** — the `run_results.json` artifact plus a post-build callback — so the
same contract works wherever dbt runs (dbt Cloud, dbt-on-Snowflake, Databricks dbt
tasks, local compose): neutrality by construction (ADR 0010/0013). This mirrors the
Airflow callback model (ADR 0007): a signed webhook is the near-real-time channel,
an artifacts poll is the 10-min fallback.

Orchestration provider, **not a datasource** (CLAUDE.md §4): this module implements
the `ConnectionAdapter` seam (config validation + connectivity test) and the
`OrchestrationProvider` seam (event parse + poll), never `CheckRunner`.

Grain (ADR 0029): the connection is a dbt **project** (one artifacts deployment,
resolved by ``project_name``); a **job** is the fine-grained trigger unit
(``pipeline_or_dag_id``), the analog of Airflow's instance→DAG. The poll reads
``<artifacts_uri>/<job>/latest/run_results.json`` per configured job.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from collections.abc import Mapping
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Any
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, field_validator, model_validator

from backend.app.core.artifacts import ArtifactTooLargeError, load_json_artifact
from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
from backend.app.orchestration.base import MalformedEventError, RunUpdate

log = get_logger(__name__)

# Fail fast rather than hang the request/beat thread on an unreachable store.
_READ_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 10.0

# The stable per-job artifact pointers the producer (upload_artifacts.py) overwrites
# every build; `runs/<UTC-ts>/` copies are retained alongside for audit/#596.
_RUN_RESULTS_RELPATH = "latest/run_results.json"
# The sibling model dependency graph, read for lineage (ADR 0034 slice 2, #759).
_MANIFEST_RELPATH = "latest/manifest.json"

# dbt node result statuses that mean the build failed (models: 'error';
# tests: 'fail'/'error'; any: 'runtime error'). Everything else — 'success',
# 'pass', 'skipped', 'warn' — is a non-failing outcome.
_DBT_FAILURE_STATUSES = frozenset({"error", "fail", "runtime error"})

# Overall-status words the callback may send (we own the snippet, but accept both
# dbt-native and DataQ-native spellings). Maps to PIPELINE_RUN_STATUSES.
_EVENT_STATUS_MAP = {
    "success": "succeeded",
    "succeeded": "succeeded",
    "pass": "succeeded",  # nosec B105 — dbt result status, not a password
    "error": "failed",
    "fail": "failed",
    "failed": "failed",
}


class DbtConfig(BaseModel):
    """Non-secret dbt orchestration-connection config (credential comes from secrets).

    Maps from ``Connection.config``. ``project_name`` resolves a run to this
    connection (``resource_config_key``). ``artifacts_uri`` is the base location of
    the dbt artifacts — ``adls://<account>/<container>/<prefix>``,
    ``s3://<bucket>/<prefix>``, or ``file:///<path>``; the poll reads
    ``<artifacts_uri>/<job>/latest/run_results.json`` for each name in ``jobs``.

    The per-connection secret is the artifacts-store read credential (ADLS SAS / S3
    secret key / unused for local). ``access_key_id``/``region`` are the non-secret
    S3 halves (required only for ``s3://``). The HMAC webhook signing key is a
    separate app-level secret (``settings.dbt_webhook_secret_name``), not here.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")

    project_name: str
    artifacts_uri: str

content.text:
Confirmed the diagnosis precisely: the **last dbt pipeline run DataQ ingested was 6 July 00:55** — the artifacts from 10, 11 and 12 July were all produced but never read. (And note the iceberg namespace now reads `postgresql+psycopg2://airflowadmin@…` with no password — the #827 fix is holding in prod.)

Now let me wait for the 10-minute beat to pick up today's build.

input.command:
TOK=$(az keyvault secret show --vault-name dataq-app-kv-aw6laj --name dataq-pat-w1-admin --query value -o tsv)
BASE=https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io
for i in $(seq 1 14); do
  N=$(curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOK" "$BASE/api/v1/pipeline_runs?limit=50" | python3 -c "
import json,sys
d=json.load(sys.stdin); items=d.get('items',d) if isinstance(d,dict) else d
runs=[r for r in items if r.get('provider')=='dbt']
new=[r for r in runs if r.get('started_at','') > '2026-07-10']
print(len(new))
")
  echo "$(date -u +%H:%M:%S)  dbt runs newer than 10 Jul: $N"
  if [ "$N" -gt 0 ]; then echo "POLL INGESTED"; break; fi
  sleep 60
done

result.output:
def _read_artifact(
    config: DbtConfig, job: str, secret: str, relpath: str = _RUN_RESULTS_RELPATH
) -> bytes | None:
    """Read ``<artifacts_uri>/<job>/<relpath>``; None if absent.

    ``relpath`` selects the per-job artifact — ``latest/run_results.json`` (the
    poll default) or ``latest/manifest.json`` (lineage, #759). Dispatches on the
    ``artifacts_uri`` scheme. Cloud SDKs are imported lazily (per
    ``core/secrets.py``) so the module — and its unit tests, which patch this
    function — never require azure/boto3. Transport/auth errors propagate (the poll
    task fails soft per connection).
    """
    parsed = urlparse(config.artifacts_uri)
    scheme = parsed.scheme

    if scheme == "file":
        from pathlib import Path

        path = Path(parsed.path) / job / relpath
        return path.read_bytes() if path.exists() else None

    if scheme == "adls":
        from azure.core.exceptions import ResourceNotFoundError
        from azure.storage.blob import BlobServiceClient

        account = parsed.netloc
        container, _, prefix = parsed.path.lstrip("/").partition("/")
        blob = f"{prefix}/{job}/{relpath}" if prefix else f"{job}/{relpath}"
        # Bound socket connect/read like the ADLS datasource adapter — `test()` runs
        # this synchronously in the request thread, so an unreachable account must
        # fail fast, not hang. (`download_blob(timeout=)` is only the server-side op
        # timeout, so set the client-level socket timeouts too.)
        client = BlobServiceClient(
            account_url=f"https://{account}.blob.core.windows.net",
            credential=secret,
            connection_timeout=int(_READ_TIMEOUT_SECONDS),
            read_timeout=int(_READ_TIMEOUT_SECONDS),
        )
        try:
            blob_bytes: bytes = (
                client.get_blob_client(container, blob)
                .download_blob(timeout=int(_READ_TIMEOUT_SECONDS))
                .readall()
            )
            return blob_bytes
        except ResourceNotFoundError:
            return None

    # s3
    import boto3
    from botocore.config import Config
    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

    bucket = parsed.netloc
    prefix = parsed.path.lstrip("/")
    key = f"{prefix}/{job}/{relpath}" if prefix else f"{job}/{relpath}"
    client = boto3.client(
        "s3",
        region_name=config.region,
        aws_access_key_id=config.access_key_id,
        aws_secret_access_key=secret,
        # Bound connect/read like the S3 datasource adapter — `test()` runs this in
        # the request thread; boto3's ~60s defaults would hang on a blackholed host.
        config=Config(
            connect_timeout=int(_READ_TIMEOUT_SECONDS), read_timeout=int(_READ_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
        ),
    )
    try:
        data: bytes = client.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=key)["Body"].read()
        return data
    except ClientError as exc:
        if exc.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code") in ("NoSuchKey", "404"):
            return None
        raise


def _parse_dt(value: Any) -> datetime | None:
    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
        return None
    try:
        return datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    except ValueError:
        return None


def _status_from_results(results: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
    """Overall run status: failed if any node failed, else succeeded (ADR 0029)."""
    for node in results:
        if str(node.get("status", "")).lower() in _DBT_FAILURE_STATUSES:
            return "failed"
    return "succeeded"

    """`ConnectionAdapter` for dbt — config validation + an artifacts-read probe."""

"""`ConnectionAdapter` for dbt — config validation + an artifacts-read probe."""

    def validate_config(self, raw: dict[str, Any]) -> DbtConfig:
        return DbtConfig.model_validate(raw)

    def test(self, raw: dict[str, Any], secret: str, **_: Any) -> None:
        """Read the first job's `latest/run_results.json`; raise on any failure.

        A green test means the artifacts store is reachable, the credential
        authenticates, and the first configured job has published a build. A
        not-yet-published job (None) is still a green test — the store and
        credential are proven; the run simply hasn't happened yet.
        """
        config = self.validate_config(raw)
        _read_artifact(config, config.jobs[0], secret)


class DbtProvider:
    """`OrchestrationProvider` for dbt — signed-callback parse + artifacts poll.

    `parse_event` consumes the JSON our `integrations/dbt/` callback POSTs (we own
    the shape): ``project_name``, ``job_name``, ``invocation_id``, ``status``
    (+ optional ``started_at`` / ``finished_at`` / ``error``). The callback is
    HMAC-authenticated over the raw body (ADR 0007/0029) and authoritative, so
    there is no REST enrichment — `fetch_run_detail` is intentionally unimplemented.
    ``invocation_id`` is the `pipeline_runs` idempotency key; it (with
    ``project_name`` / ``job_name`` / ``status``) is required.

    Both success and failure arrive on this channel; a ``succeeded`` run fires
    `trigger_bindings`. `list_recent_runs` is the poll fallback for projects that
    don't POST the callback — it reads each job's `run_results.json`.
    """

    provider = "dbt"
    resource_config_key = "project_name"

    def parse_event(self, payload: bytes, headers: Mapping[str, str]) -> RunUpdate:
        try:
            body = json.loads(payload)
        except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
            raise MalformedEventError("event body is not valid JSON") from exc
        if not isinstance(body, dict):
            raise MalformedEventError("event body must be a JSON object")

        project_name = body.get("project_name")
        job_name = body.get("job_name")
        invocation_id = body.get("invocation_id")
        raw_status = body.get("status")
        missing = [
            name
            for name, value in (
                ("project_name", project_name),
                ("job_name", job_name),
                ("invocation_id", invocation_id),
                ("status", raw_status),
            )
            if not value
        ]
        if missing:
            raise MalformedEventError(
                "event missing required field(s)", detail={"missing": missing}
            )

        status = _EVENT_STATUS_MAP.get(str(raw_status).lower())
        if status is None:
            raise MalformedEventError("unrecognised dbt run status", detail={"status": raw_status})

        return RunUpdate(
            provider_run_id=str(invocation_id),
            pipeline_or_dag_id=str(job_name),
            resource_name=str(project_name),
            status=status,
            started_at=_parse_dt(body.get("started_at")),
            finished_at=_parse_dt(body.get("finished_at")),
            failure_reason=str(body["error"]) if status == "failed" and body.get("error") else None,
        )

    def fetch_run_detail(
        self, config: Mapping[str, Any], secret: str, provider_run_id: str
    ) -> RunUpdate:
        # The signed callback / artifact is authoritative; nothing to enrich. The
        # persistence layer treats NotImplementedError as "skip enrichment".
        raise NotImplementedError("dbt artifacts are authoritative; no REST enrichment")

    def read_manifest(self, config: dict[str, Any], secret: str, job: str) -> bytes | None:
        """Read a job's ``latest/manifest.json`` for lineage (ADR 0034, #759).

        The **optional** lineage capability the orchestration_service refresh hook
        probes for via ``getattr`` — other providers (ADF / Airflow) don't have it,
        so the hook stays provider-agnostic (CLAUDE.md §11). Reads the sibling of
        ``run_results.json`` at the same per-job artifacts pointer; ``None`` when
        the manifest hasn't been published. Transport/auth errors propagate to the
        fail-open caller.
        """
        cfg = DbtConfig.model_validate(dict(config))
        return _read_artifact(cfg, job, secret, _MANIFEST_RELPATH)

    def list_recent_runs(
        self, config: Mapping[str, Any], secret: str, since: datetime
    ) -> list[RunUpdate]:
        """Poll each configured job's `latest/run_results.json`, newest-only.

        Reads `<artifacts_uri>/<job>/latest/run_results.json` per job; emits a
        `RunUpdate` when `metadata.generated_at >= since`. Trigger-on-success is
        enforced downstream (`ingest_polled_runs`); the `(provider, invocation_id)`
        upsert makes re-reading the stable `latest/` pointer idempotent. A missing

input.command: sed -n '320,375p' backend/app/orchestration/dbt.py; echo "=== since watermark:"; grep -rn "since\|_POLL_WINDOW\|lookback" backend/app/services/orchestration_service.py | head -15

result.output:
upsert makes re-reading the stable `latest/` pointer idempotent. A missing
        artifact (job not yet built) is skipped; a malformed one is skipped;
        transport/auth errors raise (the polling task fails soft per connection).
        """
        cfg = DbtConfig.model_validate(dict(config))
        updates: list[RunUpdate] = []
        for job in cfg.jobs:
            raw = _read_artifact(cfg, job, secret)
            if raw is None:
                continue
            try:
                # Shared capped+logged loader — the poll path must not `json.loads`
                # an unbounded artifact any more than the manifest parser does (#759).
                doc = load_json_artifact(raw, context=f"dbt run_results job={job}")
                metadata = doc["metadata"]
                invocation_id = metadata["invocation_id"]
                results = doc.get("results", [])
            except (ArtifactTooLargeError, ValueError, TypeError, KeyError, UnicodeDecodeError):
                # A malformed / oversized artifact for one job skips that job — but
                # now log it (the review: the silent `continue` hid a bad payload).
                log.warning("dbt_run_results_skipped", job=job, project=cfg.project_name)
                continue
            finished_at = _parse_dt(metadata.get("generated_at"))
            # `since` is always aware (UTC); only compare when generated_at parsed to
            # an aware datetime too — a tz-naive one would TypeError and fail-soft the
            # WHOLE connection poll (dropping every job), so include it rather than skip.
            if finished_at is not None and finished_at.tzinfo is not None and finished_at < since:
                continue
            updates.append(
                RunUpdate(
                    provider_run_id=str(invocation_id),
                    pipeline_or_dag_id=job,
                    resource_name=cfg.project_name,
                    status=_status_from_results(results),
                    started_at=_parse_dt(metadata.get("invocation_started_at")),
                    finished_at=finished_at,
                )
            )
        return updates

Found it. The poll is now **healthy** — it's just correctly deciding there's nothing new: `_POLL_LOOKBACK` is **15 minutes**, and today's dbt artifact was generated at 20:13, ~3 hours ago. So the poll reads it, sees `generated_at < since`, and skips.

That's right for a liveness poll, but it means the six-day backlog is unreachable — the artifact aged out of the window while auth was broken. The fix is to produce a fresh build. Let me re-run the harness dbt job.

input.command:
gh issue comment 828 --body "## Root cause confirmed, and a second defect found

**What actually broke prod lineage:** the dbt connection's artifacts-store credential (an ADLS SAS) expired on **6 Jul**. Every 10-minute poll since then failed with \`ClientAuthenticationError\`, so DataQ never read \`run_results.json\` or \`manifest.json\`. The dbt builds themselves were healthy the whole time — fresh artifacts were published on 10, 11 and 12 Jul and simply never consumed. Marquez was a red herring; the live lineage path is the dbt-manifest one (#759).

Restored today: re-minted the SAS (1y), re-ran the dbt build, poll ingested → \`refresh_dbt_lineage\` dispatched → \`lineage_edges\` populated → graph renders. The trigger-binding also fired the suite run, 4/4 green.

### Defect 1 (this issue) — a failing poll is invisible
Six days of \`orchestration_poll_failed\` produced **no user-visible signal**. It was only findable in App Insights. Worse, the UI's empty lineage state is indistinguishable from \"this asset genuinely has no upstreams\", so the outage actively looked like normal behaviour.

### Defect 2 (new, found while fixing) — the backlog is silently unrecoverable
\`_POLL_LOOKBACK\` is **15 minutes**. Once the credential was fixed, the poll started succeeding but recorded **nothing**: the newest artifact's \`generated_at\` was ~3h old, so \`finished_at < since\` skipped it. Correct for a liveness poll, but it means **any outage longer than the lookback strands every build produced during it** — the data is sitting right there in the artifact store and we refuse to read it. Recovery required manually re-running the producer.

\`gap_recovery\` (1h lookback) doesn't cover this either.

### Suggested scope
1. Surface poll health: a connection-level \`last_poll_error\` / \`last_polled_at\`, shown on the connection and on the lineage empty state (\"lineage may be stale — last dbt poll failed 6d ago\").
2. Alert on N consecutive poll failures (the \`ResultPublisher\` seam already exists).
3. Make recovery possible without touching the producer — e.g. widen gap-recovery, or ingest \`latest/\` whenever its \`invocation_id\` is one we've never seen (the \`(provider, invocation_id)\` upsert already makes that idempotent), rather than gating purely on a time window.

Credential expiry is the trigger here, but it's a generic orchestration-poll problem — ADF/Airflow have the same blind spot."
input.description: Update #828 with root cause + lookback defect

result.output: https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/828#issuecomment-4953203905

input.command:
gh issue comment 823 --body "Resolved the prod symptom that prompted this, but it was **not** an OL identity-alignment problem.

Prod lineage was empty because the dbt connection's ADLS SAS expired on 6 Jul, so the artifact poll had been failing for six days and DataQ never read \`manifest.json\`. Re-minted the credential, re-ran the build, and the dbt-manifest lineage path (#759) populated \`lineage_edges\` correctly — the graph now renders in prod with the expected 3-layer DAG (RETAIL → ANALYTICS_STG → ANALYTICS mart), identifiers matching byte-for-byte. Full write-up in #828.

So the **dbt-manifest path's identity alignment is now proven in prod**, which was half of what this issue doubted.

What remains genuinely unproven here is the **Marquez / \`LineageProvider\` pull path** (#762) — the OpenLineage emission (#758) is still dark by default, and we have not demonstrated that OL events emitted by DataQ and by an external producer land on the same \`(namespace, name)\` in a real catalog. Narrowing this issue to that." 2>&1 | tail -1
gh issue edit 823 --title "Prove OL identity alignment on the Marquez/LineageProvider pull path (dbt-manifest path now proven in prod)" 2>&1 | tail -1

gh issue create --title "Share suite is impossible on mobile: the \"Add\" button is clipped off-screen" --label bug --milestone "v1.1 Week 3" --body "## What

On a 390px-wide viewport, the **Share suite** drawer's add-collaborator row does not shrink to fit. Measured in prod (signed in, suite detail → Share):

- viewport: **390px**
- drawer body: \`clientWidth\` 390, \`scrollWidth\` **407** → overflows
- the **\"Add\" button's right edge is at x=407** → **clipped off-screen**

The user-visible consequence: **you cannot share a suite from a phone.** You can pick a collaborator and a permission, but the button that commits the grant is unreachable.

## Why

\`SharePanel.AddCollaborator\` lays the three controls out in a single non-wrapping \`<Flex gap={8}>\`:

\`\`\`tsx
<Select showSearch … style={{ flex: 1 }} />     // no minWidth: 0 → refuses to shrink
<Select value={permission} style={{ width: 110 }} />
<Button type=\"primary\">Add</Button>
\`\`\`

An antd \`Select\` has a min-content width, and \`flex: 1\` without \`min-width: 0\` cannot shrink below it. On a 342px content box the row still demands 383px, so the last child is pushed out of the drawer.

The drawer itself is fine — antd clamps it to the viewport, so it already renders as a proper full-width overlay. This is purely the body layout.

## Fix

Let the row wrap and let the search field actually shrink, so the controls reflow to two lines on a phone and stay on one line at the drawer's desktop width.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] At 390px the \"Add\" button is fully within the viewport and clickable
- [ ] The drawer body no longer overflows horizontally (\`scrollWidth <= clientWidth\`)
- [ ] The desktop layout (drawer at its configured width) is unchanged — single row
- [ ] Regression test pinning the no-overflow / button-reachable invariant" 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text: Now the friendly-label work. Let me check how the namespace is currently surfaced, and confirm the connection→namespace mapping is derivable.

input.command: grep -rn "namespace" frontend/src/pages/Assets.tsx frontend/src/pages/AssetDetail.tsx frontend/src/components/assets/LineageGraph.tsx 2>/dev/null | head -20

content.text:
Before I build this, one structural finding you need to know — it changes the answer.

**Connection names can't be the label, 1:1.** Prod has *three* Snowflake connections (`snowflake-orders`, `snowflake-payments`, `snowflake-retail`) but they all resolve to **one** namespace, `snowflake://PVQSOEQ-ZGB34383` — because the namespace is the *account*, not the connection. That's ADR 0034 working correctly (two connections to the same table must be one asset, not two). So "show the connection name" has no well-defined answer at the root.

What is well-defined is a **derived human label from the namespace itself** — pure, collision-free, no API change:

| namespace | label |
|---|---|
| `snowflake://PVQSOEQ-ZGB34383` | Snowflake · PVQSOEQ-ZGB34383 |
| `abfss://raw@dataqharness3erlgd.dfs.core.windows.net` | ADLS · dataqharness3erlgd/raw |
| `postgresql+psycopg2://airflowadmin@dataq-pg-…:5432/iceberg_catalog?sslmode=require` | Iceberg · iceberg_catalog |

The raw namespace stays the key and stays visible on hover/copy. The big win is Iceberg and ADLS, which are currently unreadable.

input.command:
gh issue create --title "Assets UI: show a human label for the OL namespace instead of the raw connection string" --label enhancement --milestone "v1.1 Week 3 — Azure wind-down + local-first posture" --body "## What

Everywhere the asset UI surfaces a datasource, it prints the raw OpenLineage \`namespace\` — which for Iceberg is a full SQLAlchemy DSN:

\`\`\`
postgresql+psycopg2://airflowadmin@<host>:5432/iceberg_catalog?sslmode=require
\`\`\`

That's the tree root in \"By source\", the namespace column in \"All assets\", the asset-detail subtitle, and every node subtitle in the lineage graph (where it's truncated to \`dev · postgresql+psycopg2://…\` and tells the reader nothing).

## Why not just show the connection name

Because a namespace is **not** 1:1 with a connection, by design (ADR 0034). Prod has three Snowflake connections (\`snowflake-orders\`, \`snowflake-payments\`, \`snowflake-retail\`) that all resolve to the single namespace \`snowflake://<account>\` — the namespace identifies the *account*, not the connection, which is exactly what makes two connections pointing at the same table resolve to **one** asset. So there is no well-defined connection name to show at a namespace root.

## What to do instead

Derive a human label from the namespace itself — a pure function, collision-free, no API change and no extra query:

| namespace | label |
|---|---|
| \`snowflake://ACCT\` | \`Snowflake · ACCT\` |
| \`unitycatalog://dbc-xxxx.cloud.databricks.com\` | \`Databricks · dbc-xxxx\` |
| \`abfss://container@account.dfs.core.windows.net\` | \`ADLS · account/container\` |
| \`s3://bucket\` | \`S3 · bucket\` |
| \`postgresql+psycopg2://user@host/iceberg_catalog?…\` | \`Iceberg · iceberg_catalog\` |
| \`file\` | \`Iceberg · local catalog\` |

The namespace stays the identity and stays reachable — tooltip on hover, plus the existing copy button on the detail page. This is presentation only; nothing about the key changes.

Side benefit: it stops printing infrastructure hostnames/usernames into the UI at a glance.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Tree roots, the flat-table column, the detail subtitle and lineage-graph node subtitles all render the label
- [ ] The raw namespace is still discoverable (tooltip / copy) and is still what the app keys on
- [ ] Unknown/unparseable namespaces fall back to the raw string rather than rendering blank
- [ ] Unit tests over the label function, incl. the Iceberg DSN and the \`file\` catalog" 2>&1 | tail -1

If you have other tasks that don't depend on this action, continue working on those. input.command:
cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ && git add -A frontend && git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(assets): read the datasource, not its connection string (#830)

Every place the asset UI surfaced a datasource, it printed the raw OpenLineage
namespace — which for Iceberg is a full SQLAlchemy DSN (driver, user, host, port,
query string). In the lineage graph, where a node subtitle has ~28 characters,
that truncated to `dev · postgresql+psy…`, which tells a reader nothing.

The namespace has to stay what it is: it's the asset's identity (ADR 0034), the
thing that makes our identifiers join byte-for-byte with dbt/Spark emissions, so
it must remain the physical, globally-unique, stable location. But nothing says it
has to be what we *print*.

So key on the namespace, show `namespaceLabel()`:

    snowflake://ACCT                        -> Snowflake · ACCT
    unitycatalog://dbc-xxxx.cloud.dbx.com   -> Databricks · dbc-xxxx
    abfss://raw@acct.dfs.core.windows.net   -> ADLS · acct/raw
    postgresql+psycopg2://u@h/iceberg_cat?… -> iceberg_cat

Note we deliberately do NOT label by connection name: a namespace is not 1:1 with
a connection — the three Snowflake connections in the harness all resolve to one
`snowflake://{account}`, and that collapse is the point (the same table reached
two ways is one asset). There is no well-defined connection name for a root.

Nor do we claim "Iceberg" for a bare URI: an Iceberg namespace is a catalog URI of
*any* scheme, so a URI proves nothing about the type. Guessing would mislabel the
first datasource that ships a URI namespace, and would contradict `datasourceKind`,
which already answers `other` here for exactly that reason. We shorten it without
naming it, and every branch falls back to the raw string rather than rendering
blank.

The raw namespace stays reachable everywhere it was: tooltip on the tree root,
table cell and detail subtitle, and it's still what the detail page's copy button
copies.

Side benefit: the UI no longer prints infrastructure hostnames and DB usernames at
a glance.

Fixes #830

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -2

## What

You could not share a suite from a phone.

The Share-suite drawer already overlays correctly on mobile — antd clamps the panel to the viewport. What broke was the row *inside* it. An antd `Select` has a min-content width that `flex: 1` alone cannot shrink past, so on a 342px content box the add-collaborator row still demanded 383px and pushed the **"Add" button to x=407 on a 390px screen**. Wrap the row and give the search field a shrinkable 160px basis, so it reflows to two lines on a phone and stays a single row at the drawer's desktop width.

input.command:
cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ
gh pr create --base fix/issue-829-share-drawer-mobile --head feat/issue-830-namespace-label --milestone "v1.1 Week 3 — Azure wind-down + local-first posture" --title "feat(assets): read the datasource, not its connection string (#830)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
> **Stacked on #831** — base is `fix/issue-829-share-drawer-mobile`. Review only the top commit (`b98e1d3d`); it retargets to `main` once #831 merges.

## What

Everywhere the asset UI surfaced a datasource, it printed the raw OpenLineage `namespace`. For Iceberg that's a full SQLAlchemy DSN — driver, user, host, port, query string. In the lineage graph, where a node subtitle has ~28 characters, it truncated to `dev · postgresql+psy…`, which tells a reader nothing.

## Why not the connection name

Because a namespace is **not** 1:1 with a connection, by design (ADR 0034). The three Snowflake connections in the harness all resolve to one `snowflake://{account}` — the namespace identifies the *account*, and that collapse is the point: the same table reached two ways must be **one** asset. So there is no well-defined connection name for a root.

## What it does

Keep the namespace as the identity (it's what makes our identifiers join byte-for-byte with dbt/Spark emissions, so it must stay physical, globally unique and stable) — and just stop *printing* it:

| namespace | label |
|---|---|
| `snowflake://ACCT` | Snowflake · ACCT |
| `unitycatalog://dbc-xxxx.cloud.databricks.com` | Databricks · dbc-xxxx |
| `abfss://raw@acct.dfs.core.windows.net` | ADLS · acct/raw |
| `s3://bucket` | S3 · bucket |
| `postgresql+psycopg2://u@h:5432/iceberg_cat?…` | iceberg_cat |
| `file` | Local catalog |

Applied to the tree root, the flat-table sub-line, the detail subtitle and the lineage-graph node subtitles. The raw namespace stays reachable in a tooltip at every site, and is still exactly what the detail page's copy button copies.

## Two things it deliberately does NOT do

- **It doesn't claim "Iceberg" for a bare URI.** An Iceberg namespace is a catalog URI of *any* scheme, so a URI proves nothing about the type. Guessing would mislabel the first datasource that ships a URI namespace, and would contradict `datasourceKind()`, which already answers `other` here for exactly that reason. So it shortens the location without naming the source. (I caught this by writing the test first: my initial implementation happily labelled `kafka://broker:9092` as "Iceberg · broker:9092".)
- **It doesn't change identity or grouping.** Tree roots are still *keyed* on the raw namespace; only the printed label changed. Two distinct namespaces cannot collapse into one root because their labels match, and the root sort is tie-broken on the namespace to stay deterministic.

Every branch falls back to the raw string rather than rendering blank — an unknown namespace degrades to "ugly but true", never to nothing.

Side benefit: the UI no longer prints infrastructure hostnames and DB usernames at a glance.

## Tests

The two nav menus are extracted into a shared `SideNav` used by both the desktop Sider and the Drawer (single DOM copy per breakpoint). Detection mechanism (Sider `onBreakpoint`) untouched, so jsdom stays desktop and the existing shell tests are unaffected. Tests: `App.test` narrow-mode block (matchMedia-driven — hamburger + Drawer overlay + close-on-nav) + Playwright `mobile-nav.spec.ts` at 390px (content width unchanged open-vs-shut, scrim closes without navigating) — [#817](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/817) | UI follow-up |
212:| ✅ | [#802](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/802) **Hierarchical asset browse** (user follow-up to #760/#773) — the Assets page gains a **By source** lens (now default) that drills **datasource → database/catalog → schema → table**, derived purely from each asset's ADR 0034 OpenLineage `namespace` + `name` (namespace = the connection-agnostic datasource root; `name` splits on `.` for warehouses / `/` for flat-file paths). Leaves open the existing AssetDetail; folders expand; env stays a per-leaf tag so DEV/QA read as distinct. The flat table is retained as the secondary **All assets** lens. Tree derivation is a pure, unit-tested module (`assetTree.ts` — namespace grouping, prefix-merge, slash/dot split, folder-and-leaf collision) kept out of the `.tsx`. **No backend change** (assets are connection-agnostic by ADR 0034). Tests: `assetTree.test` (pure) + `Assets.test` (tree default, leaf-open, folder-no-nav, table toggle) + `assets.spec` e2e (tree drill + table lens + ≥2-suite detail) — [#818](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/818) | UI follow-up |
213:| ✅ | [#803](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/803) **Asset health split — connection vs data quality** (user follow-up to #760/#773) — the single asset "Health" conflated *could DataQ reach the datasource* with *is the data good*, so a datasource DataQ couldn't connect to rendered as a data failure. Split into two derived, separately-labelled axes on AssetDetail: **Connection health** (a latest run whose execution `failed`, or any check the datasource threw on (`error`) → Errors; a `skip` (precondition unmet) → Degraded; a `cancelled` run that evaluated nothing → Cancelled; positive evidence a check was evaluated → Reachable) and **Data-quality health** (the ADR 0005 severity-weighted suite verdict; operational `error`/`skip` never rank here, per #122). Backend: `run_service.operational_result_flags` — the exact complement of `check_outcome_counts` (which drops skip/error) — rolls up to new `AssetSummary` fields `has_operational_error` / `has_skip` / `has_cancelled_run`. **Derived purely from runs already recorded — no connection-probe polling loop**, by design. Fixed three latent green-when-it-shouldn't-be bugs en route: suite health now keys "Passing" off `checks_total > 0` (evaluated checks) so a run that evaluated nothing reads **No data**, a part-way-`cancelled` run reads **Partial**, and the dashboard needs-attention rule moved to `has_operational_error` — which also catches a run that *succeeded while its checks errored*, a case the old run-status-only rule read as healthy — [#819](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/819) | UI follow-up |
214:| ✅ | [#805](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/805) **Lineage graph view** (user follow-up to #760/#761) — AssetDetail's two separate upstream/downstream list boxes become **one left-to-right DAG**: provenance left, the asset under view centred, blast radius right, **one column per hop**, nodes clickable (navigate to that asset) and keyboard-operable. The flat BFS lists answered only *what is reachable*; a graph also needs *how far* and *what connects to what*, so `lineage.edges` gained `lineage_neighbourhood` — the same depth-capped BFS, now returning each node's **hop depth** plus the **real traversed edges** (normalized upstream→downstream, cross-edges included, so the subgraph is faithful and not just a spanning tree). Surfaced as `depth` on `LineageNodeRead` + a new `lineage_edges` on `AssetDetailRead`; a depth-2 node is therefore drawn hanging off the depth-1 node it actually descends from, never off the centre it has no edge to. **No new dependency**: the layout is a layered DAG we place ourselves (pure, unit-tested `lineageLayout.ts`) rendered as inline SVG — which also makes the mobile AC trivial (the graph scrolls inside its card; the page never gains a horizontal scrollbar, pinned by an e2e assertion at 390px). ADR 0031 licence surface unchanged (zero new deps). Live-verified against the local dbt chain (RETAIL → ANALYTICS_STG → marts): crossing edges prove the edges are real, not guessed — [#820](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/820) | UI follow-up |
215:| ✅ | [#804](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/804) **Marquez stood up + the lineage pull verified end-to-end (local)** — drove the whole loop against the compose `lineage` profile: producer → Marquez → `LineageProvider` pull → `lineage_edges` → the asset graph. **Off-by-default confirmed** (`get_lineage_provider()` → `None` unconfigured); env-configured, the pull seeded from each known asset and cached **3 `source='marquez'` edges** alongside the existing 8 `source='dbt'` ones — **neither source pruned the other** (the #762 partial-index + source-scoped prune holds) — and the graph rendered the chain **including a BI-dashboard node only Marquez knew about**, which dbt's manifest never produced (the proof the pull, not dbt, fed the UI). Unknown seeds fail soft per node. **Found + fixed a runbook bug**: the old steps said "run a suite → edges appear", which is false — a DataQ run *reads* the asset it validates, so the #758 emitter sends it as a job **input** and emits no outputs, creating **no dataset→dataset edge**; edges come from whatever actually *transforms* data (dbt/Spark/Airflow OL, or any producer posting inputs+outputs). `docs/orchestration.md` now says so and shows how to post a producer chain. **Prod stand-up deferred** — the Azure subscription lapsed 2026-07-12, so there is no deployed app to attach a catalog to and Flow-A needs live Snowflake; the app side needs no further change (one env var) — [#821](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/821) | ops / G-d |

content.text:
The #830 review came back with **eight findings, and several are real** — including one that makes my own commit message false. Most important:

1. **A fifth render site I missed** — the Dashboard's "needs attention" list still prints the raw namespace.
2. **My "no usernames in the UI" claim is false** for a catalog URI with no path: `postgresql://user@host:5432` → I'd label it `user@host:5432`. The backend deliberately *keeps* the username when stripping the password, so those namespaces really do carry one.
3. **`https://rest-catalog.example.com/v1` → labelled `v1`** — meaningless, and two different REST catalogs both label `v1`.
4. **Whitespace-only namespace → blank label**, violating the module's own stated invariant.

Let me fix all of them.

input.command:
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
p='frontend/src/components/assets/assetTree.ts'
s=open(p).read()

# 1. import both from the single source of truth
s=s.replace("import { namespaceLabel } from './namespaceLabel';",
            "import { type DatasourceKind, datasourceKind, namespaceLabel } from './namespaceLabel';")

# 2. drop the duplicated kind table + type, re-export for existing importers
old = """export type DatasourceKind =
  | 'snowflake'
  | 'unity_catalog'
  | 'adls_gen2'
  | 's3'
  | 'iceberg'
  | 'other';
"""
new = """// `DatasourceKind` + `datasourceKind` live in `namespaceLabel` — one scheme table
// feeds both the icon and the label, so they can't drift apart. Re-exported here
// because this module's consumers already import them from it.
export { type DatasourceKind, datasourceKind } from './namespaceLabel';
"""
if old not in s:
    # the file may hold the single-line form
    old = "export type DatasourceKind =\n  'snowflake' | 'unity_catalog' | 'adls_gen2' | 's3' | 'iceberg' | 'other';\n"
assert old in s, "kind type block not found"
s = s.replace(old, new)

# 3. remove the now-duplicated datasourceKind function
import re
s = re.sub(
    r"/\*\* Classify an OL namespace by its scheme, for the root-node icon/label\. \*/\nexport function datasourceKind\(namespace: string\): DatasourceKind \{.*?\n\}\n\n",
    "",
    s,
    flags=re.S,
)

# 4. label is a plain string now
s = s.replace("label: namespaceLabel(asset.namespace).text,", "label: namespaceLabel(asset.namespace),")
open(p,'w').write(s)
print("ok")
PYEOF

fixup: make the mobile share spec actually pin the bug (#829 review)

The agentic review found the e2e spec could go GREEN against the unfixed code —
which would have made it worse than no test at all:

- `toBeInViewport()` defaults to `ratio: 0` ("intersects the viewport at all").
  Pre-fix the Add button spanned x≈348→407 on a 390px viewport, so ~71% of it was
  on screen and the default assertion PASSED on the bug. Now `ratio: 1`.
- The overflow measurement ran before `AsyncBody` resolved, so it could measure a
  bare <Spin> (which of course doesn't overflow). Now it waits for the row, and
  polls the measurement across the drawer's slide-in.
- `getByText('Can view')` was a latent strict-mode landmine: `ShareRow` renders the
  same permission labels, so it goes ambiguous the moment this suite has a view
  share. Scoped to the add row.

Also adopt the existing sibling pattern: TriggersPanel and SchedulesPanel already
use `<Flex wrap>` + `{ flex: 1, minWidth: N }` for the same shape — this row was
simply the one that never got it. The `1 1 160px` basis was redundant next to an
explicit `minWidth`. And the comment described a wrap that doesn't happen (the
permission picker stays on line 1; only Add wraps) — corrected against the measured
layout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push -q origin fix/issue-829-share-drawer-mobile && git log --oneline -1

git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fixup: fix the label's own leaks and blind spots (#830 review)

The agentic review found four real defects, two of which made the commit message's
own claims false:

- **A fifth render site was missed.** The dashboard's "needs attention" list
  (AssetHealthLead) still printed the raw namespace, so the exact failure #830
  describes — an Iceberg DSN ellipsed to `postgresql+psycopg2://user@pg-…` — was
  still live on the page a user sees first.
- **The label leaked the DB username.** With no database path to fall back on
  (`postgresql://user@host:5432`), the generic branch used the raw authority, which
  still carried `user@`. This is not hypothetical: the backend's
  `strip_uri_credentials` deliberately KEEPS the username when stripping the
  password, so namespaces genuinely arrive carrying one. Userinfo is now always
  dropped.
- **REST catalogs labelled `v1`.** Treating the last path segment as "the database"
  is a driver-DSN assumption; for an Iceberg REST catalog (`https://host/v1`) the
  path is an API route, so two different catalogs both read `v1` — meaningless and
  identical. Split on the scheme: for http/https/thrift/grpc the host IS the
  catalog; only a driver DSN has a database in its path.
- **A whitespace-only namespace rendered blank**, violating the module's own stated
  invariant (`trim()` ran before the emptiness guard, so the raw string was thrown
  away instead of returned).

Also folded in the review's structural points: `datasourceKind` and `namespaceLabel`
were two hand-synced prefix tables — one scheme table now feeds both, so a new
scheme can't produce an `S3 ·` label under an `other` icon; matching is
case-insensitive; and the `NamespaceLabel` object was dead API surface (every call
site read `.text`), so it returns a plain string.

Tests gain the adversarial battery the review asked for — the hostile inputs are
now the point, not seven happy paths (CONTRIBUTING rule 4a): every degenerate
namespace asserted non-blank, the userinfo cases asserted not to contain the
username, and the two REST catalogs asserted distinguishable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -1
